header.title: God's Firstborn Son
header.description: These are verses related to God's firstborn son Israel

blocks.title: God calls Israel his firstborn son

texts.NIV84: Then say to Pharaoh, 'This is what the LORD says: Israel is my firstborn son,
texts.KJV: And thou shalt say unto Pharaoh, Thus saith the Lord, Israel is my son, even my firstborn:
texts.NASB95: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the Lord, “Israel is My son, My firstborn.
texts.NKJV: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the Lord: “Israel is My son, My firstborn.
texts.DRB: And thou shalt say to him: Thus saith the Lord: Israel is my son, my firstborn.
texts.ESV: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the Lord, Israel is my firstborn son,
texts.LEB: And you must say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says Yahweh, “Israel is my son, my firstborn.”
texts.AMP: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the LORD, “Israel is My son, My firstborn.
texts.NLT: Then you will tell him, ‘This is what the Lord says: Israel is my firstborn son.
texts.AKJV: And thou shalt say unto Pharaoh, Thus saith the Lord, Israel is my son, even my firstborn:
texts.NRSV: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the Lord: Israel is my firstborn son.

texts.NIV84: and I told you, "Let my son go, so he may worship me." But you refused to let him go; so I will kill your firstborn son.'"
texts.KJV: And I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
texts.NASB95: So I said to you, ‘Let My son go that he may serve Me’; but you have refused to let him go. Behold, I will kill your son, your firstborn.”’”
texts.NKJV: So I say to you, let My son go that he may serve Me. But if you refuse to let him go, indeed I will kill your son, your firstborn.” ’ ”
texts.DRB: I have said to thee: Let my son go, that he may serve me, and thou wouldst not let him go: behold I will kill thy son, thy firstborn.
texts.ESV: and I say to you, “Let my son go that he may serve me.” If you refuse to let him go, behold, I will kill your firstborn son.’”
texts.LEB: And I said to you, “Release my son and let him serve me,” but you refused to release him. Look, I am about to kill your son, your firstborn.’”
texts.AMP: So I say to you, ‘Let My son go so that he may serve Me’; and if you refuse to let him go, behold, I will kill your son, your firstborn.”’”
texts.NLT: I commanded you, “Let my son go, so he can worship me.” But since you have refused, I will now kill your firstborn son!’”
texts.AKJV: and I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
texts.NRSV: I said to you, “Let my son go that he may serve me.” But you refused to let him go; now I will kill your firstborn son.’ ”

texts.NIV84: "When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.
texts.KJV: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.
texts.NASB95:
When Israel was a youth I loved him,
And out of Egypt I called My son.
texts.NKJV:
“When Israel was a child, I loved him,
And out of Egypt I called My son.
texts.DRB: As the morning passeth, so hath the king of Israel Israel was a child, and I loved him: and I called my son out of Egypt.
texts.ESV:
When Israel was a child, I loved him,
    and out of Egypt I called my son.
texts.LEB:
When Israel was a child, I loved him,
and out of Egypt I called my son.
texts.AMP: When Israel was a child [a young nation], I loved him, And I called My son out of Egypt.
texts.NLT:
“When Israel was a child, I loved him,
    and I called my son out of Egypt.
texts.AKJV:
When Israel was a child, then I loved him,
and called my son out of Egypt.
texts.NRSV:
When Israel was a child, I loved him,
    and out of Egypt I called my son.

texts.NIV84: I will tell of the kindnesses of the LORD, the deeds for which he is to be praised, according to all the LORD has done for us- yes, the many good things he has done for the house of Israel, according to his compassion and many kindnesses.
texts.KJV: I will mention the lovingkindnesses of the Lord, and the praises of the Lord, according to all that the Lord hath bestowed on us, and the great goodness toward the house of Israel, which he hath bestowed on them according to his mercies, and according to the multitude of his lovingkindnesses.
texts.NASB95:
I shall make mention of the lovingkindnesses of the Lord, the praises of the Lord,
According to all that the Lord has granted us,
And the great goodness toward the house of Israel,
Which He has granted them according to His compassion
And according to the abundance of His lovingkindnesses.
texts.NKJV:
I will mention the lovingkindnesses of the Lord
And the praises of the Lord,
According to all that the Lord has bestowed on us,
And the great goodness toward the house of Israel,
Which He has bestowed on them according to His mercies,
According to the multitude of His lovingkindnesses.
texts.DRB: I will remember the tender mercies of the Lord, the praise of the Lord for all the things that the Lord hath bestowed upon us, and for the multitude of his good things to the house of Israel, which he hath given them according to his kindness, and according to the multitude of his mercies.
texts.ESV:
I will recount the steadfast love of the Lord,
    the praises of the Lord,
according to all that the Lord has granted us,
    and the great goodness to the house of Israel
that he has granted them according to his compassion,
    according to the abundance of his steadfast love.
texts.LEB:
I will mention the loyal love of Yahweh, the praises of Yahweh, according to all that Yahweh has done for us,
    and the greatness of goodness to the house of Israel that he has done to them according to his mercy and the abundance of his loyal love.
texts.AMP: I will tell of the lovingkindnesses of the LORD, and the praiseworthy deeds of the LORD, According to all that the LORD has done for us, And His great goodness toward the house of Israel, Which He has shown them according to His compassion And according to the abundance of His lovingkindnesses.
texts.NLT:
I will tell of the Lord’s unfailing love.
    I will praise the Lord for all he has done.
I will rejoice in his great goodness to Israel,
    which he has granted according to his mercy and love.
texts.AKJV:
I will mention the lovingkindnesses of the Lord,
and the praises of the Lord,
according to all that the Lord hath bestowed on us,
and the great goodness toward the house of Israel,
which he hath bestowed on them according to his mercies,
and according to the multitude of his lovingkindnesses.
texts.NRSV:
I will recount the gracious deeds of the Lord,
    the praiseworthy acts of the Lord,
because of all that the Lord has done for us
    and the great favor to the house of Israel
that he has shown them according to his mercy,
    according to the abundance of his steadfast love.

texts.NIV84: He said, "Surely they are my people, sons who will not be false to me"; and so he became their Savior.
texts.KJV: For he said, Surely they are my people, children that will not lie: so he was their Saviour.
texts.NASB95:
For He said, “Surely, they are My people,
Sons who will not deal falsely.”
So He became their Savior.
texts.NKJV:
For He said, “Surely they are My people,
Children who will not lie.”
So He became their Savior.
texts.DRB: And he said: Surely they are my people, children that will not deny: so he became their saviour.
texts.ESV:
For he said, “Surely they are my people,
    children who will not deal falsely.”
    And he became their Savior.
texts.LEB: And he said, “Surely my people are children; they will not break faith.” And he became a Savior to them.
texts.AMP: For He said, “Be assured, they are My people, Sons who will not be faithless.” So He became their Savior [in all their distresses].
texts.NLT:
He said, “They are my very own people.
    Surely they will not betray me again.”
    And he became their Savior.
texts.AKJV:
For he said, Surely they are my people,
children that will not lie:
so he was their Saviour.
texts.NRSV:
For he said, “Surely they are my people,
    children who will not act deceitfully,”
and he became their savior

texts.NIV84: In all their distress he too was distressed, and the angel of his presence saved them. In his love and mercy he redeemed them; he lifted them up and carried them all the days of old.
texts.KJV: In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them: in his love and in his pity he redeemed them; and he bare them, and carried them all the days of old.
texts.NASB95:
In all their affliction He was afflicted,
And the angel of His presence saved them;
In His love and in His mercy He redeemed them,
And He lifted them and carried them all the days of old.
texts.DRB: In all their affliction he was not troubled, and the angel of his presence saved them: in his love, and in his mercy he redeemed them, and he carried them and lifted them up all the days of old.
texts.LEB:
In all their distress, there was no distress,
    and the messenger of his presence saved them,
    in his love and compassion he himself redeemed them,
and he lifted them up,
    and he supported them all the days of old.
texts.AMP: In all their distress He was distressed, And the angel of His presence saved them, In His love and in His compassion He redeemed them; And He lifted them up and carried them all the days of old.
texts.NLT:
In all their suffering he also suffered,
    and he personally rescued them.
In his love and mercy he redeemed them.
    He lifted them up and carried them
    through all the years.
texts.AKJV:
In all their affliction he was afflicted,
and the angel of his presence saved them:
in his love and in his pity he redeemed them;
and he bare them, and carried them all the days of old.

texts.NIV84: But you are our Father, though Abraham does not know us or Israel acknowledge us; you, O LORD, are our Father, our Redeemer from of old is your name.
texts.KJV: Doubtless thou art our father, though Abraham be ignorant of us, and Israel acknowledge us not: thou, O Lord, art our father, our redeemer; thy name is from everlasting.
texts.NASB95:
For You are our Father, though Abraham does not know us
And Israel does not recognize us.
You, O Lord, are our Father,
Our Redeemer from of old is Your name.
texts.NKJV:
Doubtless You are our Father,
Though Abraham was ignorant of us,
And Israel does not acknowledge us.
You, O Lord, are our Father;
Our Redeemer from Everlasting is Your name.
texts.DRB: For thou art our father, and Abraham hath not known us, and Israel hath been ignorant of us: thou, O Lord, art our father, our redeemer, from everlasting is thy name.
texts.ESV:
For you are our Father,
    though Abraham does not know us,
    and Israel does not acknowledge us;
you, O Lord, are our Father,
    our Redeemer from of old is your name.
texts.LEB:
For you are our father, although Abraham does not know us,
    and Israel does not acknowledge us.
You, Yahweh are our father,
    Our Redeemer from of old is your name.
texts.AMP: For [most certainly] You are our Father, even though Abraham [our ancestor] does not know us And Israel does not acknowledge us. You, O LORD, are [still] our Father, Our Redeemer from everlasting is Your name.
texts.NLT:
Surely you are still our Father!
    Even if Abraham and Jacob would disown us,
Lord, you would still be our Father.
    You are our Redeemer from ages past.
texts.AKJV:
Doubtless thou art our father,
though Abraham be ignorant of us,
and Israel acknowledge us not:
thou, O Lord, art our father, our redeemer;
thy name is from everlasting.

texts.NIV84: Why, O LORD, do you make us wander from your ways and harden our hearts so we do not revere you? Return for the sake of your servants, the tribes that are your inheritance.
texts.KJV: O Lord, why hast thou made us to err from thy ways, and hardened our heart from thy fear? Return for thy servants' sake, the tribes of thine inheritance.
texts.NASB95:
Why, O Lord, do You cause us to stray from Your ways
And harden our heart from fearing You?
Return for the sake of Your servants, the tribes of Your heritage.
texts.NKJV:
O Lord, why have You made us stray from Your ways,
And hardened our heart from Your fear?
Return for Your servants’ sake,
The tribes of Your inheritance.
texts.DRB: Why hast thou made us to err, O Lord, from thy ways: why hast thou hardened our heart, that we should not fear thee? return for the sake of thy servants, the tribes of thy inheritance.
texts.ESV:
O Lord, why do you make us wander from your ways
    and harden our heart, so that we fear you not?
Return for the sake of your servants,
    the tribes of your heritage.
texts.LEB:
Why do you make us wander, Yahweh?
    You harden our heart from your ways so that we do not fear you.
Turn back for the sake of your servants,
    the tribes of your inheritance.
texts.AMP: O LORD, why do You cause us to stray from Your ways And harden our heart from fearing You [with reverence and awe]? Return for Your servants’ sake, the tribes of Your heritage.
texts.NLT:
Lord, why have you allowed us to turn from your path?
    Why have you given us stubborn hearts so we no longer fear you?
Return and help us, for we are your servants,
    the tribes that are your special possession.
texts.AKJV:
O Lord, why hast thou made us to err from thy ways,
and hardened our heart from thy fear?
Return for thy servants’ sake,
the tribes of thine inheritance.
texts.NRSV:
Why, O Lord, do you let us stray from your ways
    and let our heart harden, so that we do not fear you?
Turn back for the sake of your servants,
    the tribes that are your heritage.

texts.NIV84: The LORD appeared to us in the past, saying: "I have loved you with an everlasting love; I have drawn you with loving-kindness.
texts.KJV: The Lord hath appeared of old unto me, saying, Yea, I have loved thee with an everlasting love: therefore with lovingkindness have I drawn thee.
texts.DRB: The Lord hath appeared from afar to me. Yea I have loved thee with everlasting love, therefore have I drawn thee, taking pity on thee.
texts.LEB:
From afar Yahweh appeared to me, saying,
“I have loved you with an everlasting love.
    Therefore I have drawn you with loyal love.
texts.AMP: The LORD appeared to me (Israel) from ages past, saying, “I have loved you with an everlasting love; Therefore with lovingkindness I have drawn you and continued My faithfulness to you.
texts.NLT:
Long ago the Lord said to Israel:
“I have loved you, my people, with an everlasting love.
    With unfailing love I have drawn you to myself.
texts.AKJV:
The Lord hath appeared of old unto me, saying,
Yea, I have loved thee with an everlasting love:
therefore with lovingkindness have I drawn thee.

texts.NIV84: I will build you up again and you will be rebuilt, O Virgin Israel. Again you will take up your tambourines and go out to dance with the joyful.
texts.KJV: Again I will build thee, and thou shalt be built, O virgin of Israel: thou shalt again be adorned with thy tabrets, and shalt go forth in the dances of them that make merry.
texts.NASB95:
“Again I will build you and you will be rebuilt,
O virgin of Israel!
Again you will take up your tambourines,
And go forth to the dances of the merrymakers.
texts.NKJV:
Again I will build you, and you shall be rebuilt,
O virgin of Israel!
You shall again be adorned with your tambourines,
And shall go forth in the dances of those who rejoice.
texts.DRB: And I will build thee again, and thou shalt be built, 0 virgin of Israel: thou shalt again be adorned with thy timbrels, and shalt go forth in the dances of them that make merry.
texts.ESV:
Again I will build you, and you shall be built,
    O virgin Israel!
Again you shall adorn yourself with tambourines
    and shall go forth in the dance of the merrymakers.
texts.LEB:
I will again build you,
    and you will be built, O virgin Israel.
You will again adorn yourself with your tambourines,
    and you will go forth in the dancing in a ring of the merrymakers.
texts.AMP: “Again I will build you and you will be rebuilt, O Virgin Israel! You will again be adorned with your tambourines and timbrels And go out to the dances of those who celebrate.
texts.NLT:
I will rebuild you, my virgin Israel.
    You will again be happy
    and dance merrily with your tambourines.
texts.AKJV:
Again I will build thee, and thou shalt be built,
O virgin of Israel:
thou shalt again be adorned with thy tabrets,
and shalt go forth in the dances of them that make merry.
texts.NRSV:
Again I will build you, and you shall be built,
    O virgin Israel!
Again you shall adorn yourself with your tambourines
    and go forth in the dance of the merrymakers.

texts.NIV84: They will come with weeping; they will pray as I bring them back. I will lead them beside streams of water on a level path where they will not stumble, because I am Israel's father, and Ephraim is my firstborn son.
texts.KJV: They shall come with weeping, and with supplications will I lead them: I will cause them to walk by the rivers of waters in a straight way, wherein they shall not stumble: for I am a father to Israel, and Ephraim is my firstborn.
texts.NKJV:
They shall come with weeping,
And with supplications I will lead them.
I will cause them to walk by the rivers of waters,
In a straight way in which they shall not stumble;
For I am a Father to Israel,
And Ephraim is My firstborn.
texts.DRB: They shall come with weeping: and I will bring them back in mercy: and I will bring them through the torrents of waters in a right way, and they shall not stumble in it: for I am a father to Israel, and Ephraim is my firstborn.
texts.ESV:
With weeping they shall come,
    and with pleas for mercy I will lead them back,
I will make them walk by brooks of water,
    in a straight path in which they shall not stumble,
for I am a father to Israel,
    and Ephraim is my firstborn.
texts.AMP: “They will come with weeping [in repentance and for joy], And by [their] prayer [for the future] I will lead them; I will make them walk by streams of waters, On a straight path in which they will not stumble, For I am a Father to Israel, And Ephraim (Israel) is My firstborn.”
texts.NLT:
Tears of joy will stream down their faces,
    and I will lead them home with great care.
They will walk beside quiet streams
    and on smooth paths where they will not stumble.
For I am Israel’s father,
    and Ephraim is my oldest child.
texts.AKJV:
They shall come with weeping,
and with supplications will I lead them:
I will cause them to walk by the rivers of waters in a straight way,
wherein they shall not stumble:
for I am a father to Israel,
and Ephraim is my firstborn.

texts.NIV84: "Hear the word of the LORD, O nations; proclaim it in distant coastlands: 'He who scattered Israel will gather them and will watch over his flock like a shepherd.'
texts.KJV: Hear the word of the Lord, O ye nations, and declare it in the isles afar off, and say, He that scattered Israel will gather him, and keep him, as a shepherd doth his flock.
texts.NASB95:
Hear the word of the Lord, O nations,
And declare in the coastlands afar off,
And say, “He who scattered Israel will gather him
And keep him as a shepherd keeps his flock.”
texts.NKJV:
“Hear the word of the Lord, O nations,
And declare it in the isles afar off, and say,
‘He who scattered Israel will gather him,
And keep him as a shepherd does his flock.’
texts.DRB: Hear the word of the Lord, O ye nations, and declare it in the islands that are afar off, and say: He that scattered Israel will gather him: and he will keep him as the shepherd doth his flock.
texts.ESV:
“Hear the word of the Lord, O nations,
    and declare it in the coastlands far away;
say, ‘He who scattered Israel will gather him,
    and will keep him as a shepherd keeps his flock.’
texts.LEB:
Hear the word of Yahweh, O nations,
    and declare in the coastlands from afar, and say,
“The scatterer of Israel will gather him,
    and he will keep him as a shepherd his flock.
texts.AMP: Hear the word of the LORD, O you nations, And declare it in the isles and coastlands far away, And say, “He who scattered Israel will gather him And will keep him as a shepherd keeps his flock.”
texts.NLT:
“Listen to this message from the Lord,
    you nations of the world;
    proclaim it in distant coastlands:
The Lord, who scattered his people,
    will gather them and watch over them
    as a shepherd does his flock.
texts.AKJV:
Hear the word of the Lord, O ye nations,
and declare it in the isles afar off, and say,
He that scattered Israel will gather him, and keep him,
as a shepherd doth his flock.
texts.NRSV:
Hear the word of the Lord, O nations,
    and declare it in the coastlands far away;
say, “He who scattered Israel will gather him
    and will keep him as a shepherd does a flock.”

texts.NIV84: Is not Ephraim my dear son, the child in whom I delight? Though I often speak against him, I still remember him. Therefore my heart yearns for him; I have great compassion for him," declares the LORD.
texts.KJV: Is Ephraim my dear son? is he a pleasant child? for since I spake against him, I do earnestly remember him still: therefore my bowels are troubled for him; I will surely have mercy upon him, saith the Lord.
texts.DRB: Surely Ephraim is an honourable son to me, surely he is a tender child: for since I spoke of him, I will still remember him. Therefore are my bowels troubled for him: pitying I will pity him, saith the Lord.
texts.ESV:
Is Ephraim my dear son?
    Is he my darling child?
For as often as I speak against him,
    I do remember him still.
Therefore my heart yearns for him;
    I will surely have mercy on him,
declares the Lord.
texts.LEB:
Is Ephraim my dear son,
    or the child of my delight?
For as often as I have earnestly spoken against him,
    I still remember him.
Therefore my bowels are turbulent for him,
    surely I will have compassion on him,” declares Yahweh.
texts.AMP: “Is Ephraim My dear son? Is he a darling and beloved child? For as often as I have spoken against him, I certainly still remember him. Therefore My affection is renewed and My heart longs for him; I will surely have mercy on him,” says the LORD.
texts.NLT:
“Is not Israel still my son,
    my darling child?” says the Lord.
“I often have to punish him,
    but I still love him.
That’s why I long for him
    and surely will have mercy on him.
texts.NRSV:
Is Ephraim my dear son?
    Is he the child in whom I delight?
As often as I speak against him,
    I still remember him.
Therefore I am deeply moved for him;
    I will surely have mercy on him,
            says the Lord.

texts.NIV84: "Set up road signs; put up guideposts. Take note of the highway, the road that you take. Return, O Virgin Israel, return to your towns.
texts.KJV: Set thee up waymarks, make thee high heaps: set thine heart toward the highway, even the way which thou wentest: turn again, O virgin of Israel, turn again to these thy cities.
texts.NASB95:
“Set up for yourself roadmarks,
Place for yourself guideposts;
Direct your mind to the highway,
The way by which you went.
Return, O virgin of Israel,
Return to these your cities.
texts.NKJV:
“Set up signposts,
Make landmarks;
Set your heart toward the highway,
The way in which you went.
Turn back, O virgin of Israel,
Turn back to these your cities.
texts.DRB: Set thee up a watchtower, make to thee bitterness: direct thy heart into the right way, wherein thou hast walked: return, O virgin of Israel, return to these thy cities.
texts.LEB:
“Set up for yourself road markers,
    make for yourself signposts,
set your mind to the main road,
    the road by which you went.
Return, O virgin of Israel,
    return to these your cities.
texts.AMP: “Place for yourself road signs [toward Canaan], Make for yourself guideposts; Turn your thought and attention to the highway, To the way by which you went [into exile]. Retrace your steps, O virgin of Israel, Return to these your cities.
texts.AKJV:
Set thee up waymarks, make thee high heaps:
set thine heart toward the highway,
even the way which thou wentest:
turn again, O virgin of Israel,
turn again to these thy cities.

texts.NIV84: How long will you wander, O unfaithful daughter? The LORD will create a new thing on earth- a woman will surround a man."
texts.KJV: How long wilt thou go about, O thou backsliding daughter? for the Lord hath created a new thing in the earth, A woman shall compass a man.
texts.NASB95:
“How long will you go here and there,
O faithless daughter?
For the Lord has created a new thing in the earth—
A woman will encompass a man.”
texts.NKJV:
How long will you gad about,
O you backsliding daughter?
For the Lord has created a new thing in the earth—
A woman shall encompass a man.”
texts.DRB: How long wilt thou be dissolute in deliciousness, O wandering daughter? for the Lord hath created a new thing upon the earth: A WOMAN SHALL COMPASS A MAN.
texts.ESV:
How long will you waver,
    O faithless daughter?
For the Lord has created a new thing on the earth:
    a woman encircles a man.”
texts.LEB:
How long will you waver, O unfaithful daughter?
For Yahweh has created a new thing on the earth,
    a woman, she shelters a man.”
texts.AMP: “How long will you hesitate [to return], O you faithless and renegade daughter? For the LORD has created a new thing in the land [of Israel]: A woman will encompass (tenderly love) a man.”
texts.NLT:
How long will you wander,
    my wayward daughter?
For the Lord will cause something new to happen—
    Israel will embrace her God.”
texts.AKJV:
How long wilt thou go about,
O thou backsliding daughter?
for the Lord hath created a new thing in the earth,
A woman shall compass a man.
texts.NRSV:
How long will you waver,
    O faithless daughter?
For the Lord has created a new thing on the earth:
    a woman encompasses a man.

texts.NIV84: You are the children of the LORD your God. Do not cut yourselves or shave the front of your heads for the dead,
texts.KJV: Ye are the children of the Lord your God: ye shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.
texts.NASB95: “You are the sons of the Lord your God; you shall not cut yourselves nor shave your forehead for the sake of the dead.
texts.NKJV: “You are the children of the Lord your God; you shall not cut yourselves nor shave the front of your head for the dead.
texts.DRB: Be ye children of the Lord your God: you shall not cut yourselves, no, make any baldness for the dead;
texts.ESV: “You are the sons of the Lord your God. You shall not cut yourselves or make any baldness on your foreheads for the dead.
texts.LEB: “You are children of Yahweh your God; therefore you must not gash yourself, and you must not make your forehead bald for the dead.
texts.AMP: “You are the sons of the LORD your God; you shall not cut yourselves nor shave your forehead for the sake of the dead,
texts.NLT: “Since you are the people of the Lord your God, never cut yourselves or shave the hair above your foreheads in mourning for the dead.
texts.AKJV: Ye are the children of the Lord your God: ye shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.
texts.NRSV: “You are children of the Lord your God. You must not lacerate yourselves or shave your forelocks for the dead.

texts.NIV84: for you are a people holy to the LORD your God. Out of all the peoples on the face of the earth, the LORD has chosen you to be his treasured possession.
texts.KJV: For thou art an holy people unto the Lord thy God, and the Lord hath chosen thee to be a peculiar people unto himself, above all the nations that are upon the earth.
texts.NASB95: For you are a holy people to the Lord your God, and the Lord has chosen you to be a people for His own possession out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
texts.NKJV: For you are a holy people to the Lord your God, and the Lord has chosen you to be a people for Himself, a special treasure above all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
texts.DRB: Because thou art a holy people to the Lord thy God: and he chose thee to be his peculiar people of all nations that are upon the earth.
texts.ESV: For you are a people holy to the Lord your God, and the Lord has chosen you to be a people for his treasured possession, out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
texts.LEB: For you are a people holy to Yahweh your God, and you Yahweh has chosen to be a treasured possession from among all of the peoples that are on the surface of the earth.
texts.AMP: for you are a holy people [set apart] to the LORD your God; and the LORD has chosen you out of all the peoples who are on the earth to be a people for His own possession.
texts.NLT: You have been set apart as holy to the Lord your God, and he has chosen you from all the nations of the earth to be his own special treasure.
texts.AKJV: For thou art an holy people unto the Lord thy God, and the Lord hath chosen thee to be a peculiar people unto himself, above all the nations that are upon the earth.
texts.NRSV: For you are a people holy to the Lord your God; it is you the Lord has chosen out of all the peoples on earth to be his people, his treasured possession.

texts.NIV84: Is this the way you repay the LORD, O foolish and unwise people? Is he not your Father, your Creator, who made you and formed you?
texts.KJV: Do ye thus requite the Lord, O foolish people and unwise? is not he thy father that hath bought thee? hath he not made thee, and established thee?
texts.NASB95:
“Do you thus repay the Lord,
O foolish and unwise people?
Is not He your Father who has bought you?
He has made you and established you.
texts.NKJV:
Do you thus deal with the Lord,
O foolish and unwise people?
Is He not your Father, who bought you?
Has He not made you and established you?
texts.DRB: Is this the return thou makest to the Lord, O foolish and senseless people? Is not he thy father, that hath possessed thee, and made thee, and created thee?
texts.ESV:
Do you thus repay the Lord,
    you foolish and senseless people?
Is not he your father, who created you,
    who made you and established you?
texts.LEB:
Like this do you treat Yahweh,
    foolish and unwise people?
Has he not, your father, created you?
    He made you, and he established you.
texts.AMP: “Do you thus repay the LORD, O foolish and unwise people? Is not He your Father who has acquired you [as His own]? He has made you and established you [as a nation].
texts.NLT:
Is this the way you repay the Lord,
    you foolish and senseless people?
Isn’t he your Father who created you?
    Has he not made you and established you?
texts.AKJV:
Do ye thus requite the Lord, Ofoolish people and unwise?
is not he thy father that hath bought thee?
hath he not made thee, and established thee?
texts.NRSV:
Do you thus repay the Lord,
    O foolish and senseless people?
Is not he your father who created you,
    who made you and established you?

texts.NIV84: Remember the days of old; consider the generations long past. Ask your father and he will tell you, your elders, and they will explain to you.
texts.KJV: Remember the days of old, consider the years of many generations: ask thy father, and he will shew thee; thy elders, and they will tell thee.
texts.NASB95:
“Remember the days of old,
Consider the years of all generations.
Ask your father, and he will inform you,
Your elders, and they will tell you.
texts.NKJV:
“Remember the days of old,
Consider the years of many generations.
Ask your father, and he will show you;
Your elders, and they will tell you:
texts.DRB: Remember the days of old, think upon every generation: ask thy father, and he will declare to thee: thy elders and they will tell thee.
texts.AMP: “Remember the days of old, Consider the years of many generations. Ask your father, and he will inform you, Your elders, and they will tell you.
texts.NLT:
Remember the days of long ago;
    think about the generations past.
Ask your father, and he will inform you.
    Inquire of your elders, and they will tell you.
texts.AKJV:
Remember the days of old,
consider the years of many generations:
ask thy father, and he will shew thee;
thy elders, and they will tell thee.

texts.NIV84: When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance, when he divided all mankind, he set up boundaries for the peoples according to the number of the sons of Israel.
texts.KJV: When the Most High divided to the nations their inheritance, when he separated the sons of Adam, he set the bounds of the people according to the number of the children of Israel.
texts.NASB95:
“When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance,
When He separated the sons of man,
He set the boundaries of the peoples
According to the number of the sons of Israel.
texts.NKJV:
When the Most High divided their inheritance to the nations,
When He separated the sons of Adam,
He set the boundaries of the peoples
According to the number of the children of Israel.
texts.DRB: When the Most High divided the nations: when he separated the sons of Adam, he appointed the bounds of people according to the number of the children of Israel.
texts.ESV:
When the Most High gave to the nations their inheritance,
    when he divided mankind,
he fixed the borders of the peoples
    according to the number of the sons of God.
texts.LEB:
When the Most High apportioned the nations,
    at his dividing up of the sons of humankind,
he fixed the boundaries of the peoples,
    according to the number of the children of Israel.
texts.AMP: “When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance, When He separated the sons of man, He set the boundaries of the peoples According to the number of the sons of Israel.
texts.NLT:
When the Most High assigned lands to the nations,
    when he divided up the human race,
he established the boundaries of the peoples
    according to the number in his heavenly court.
texts.AKJV:
When the most High divided to the nations their inheritance,
when he separated the sons of Adam,
he set the bounds of the people according to the number of the children of Israel.
texts.NRSV:
When the Most High apportioned the nations,
    when he divided humankind,
he fixed the boundaries of the peoples
    according to the number of the gods;

texts.NIV84: For the LORD's portion is his people, Jacob his allotted inheritance.
texts.KJV: For the Lord's portion is his people; Jacob is the lot of his inheritance.
texts.NASB95:
“For the Lord’s portion is His people;
Jacob is the allotment of His inheritance.
texts.NKJV:
For the Lord’s portion is His people;
Jacob is the place of His inheritance.
texts.DRB: But the Lord's portion is his people: Jacob the lot of his inheritance.
texts.ESV:
But the Lord's portion is his people,
    Jacob his allotted heritage.
texts.LEB:
For Yahweh’s portion was his people,
    Jacob the share of his inheritance.
texts.AMP: “For the LORD’S portion and chosen share is His people; Jacob (Israel) is the allotment of His inheritance.
texts.NLT:
“For the people of Israel belong to the Lord;
    Jacob is his special possession.
texts.AKJV:
For the Lord’s portion is his people;
Jacob is the lot of his inheritance.
texts.NRSV:
the Lord’s own portion was his people,
    Jacob his allotted share.

texts.NIV84: The LORD saw this and rejected them because he was angered by his sons and daughters.
texts.KJV: And when the Lord saw it, he abhorred them, because of the provoking of his sons, and of his daughters.
texts.NASB95:
“The Lord saw this, and spurned them
Because of the provocation of His sons and daughters.
texts.NKJV:
“And when the Lord saw it, He spurned them,
Because of the provocation of His sons and His daughters.
texts.DRB: The Lord saw, and was moved to wrath: because his own sons and daughters provoked him.
texts.ESV:
“The Lord saw it and spurned them,
    because of the provocation of his sons and his daughters.
texts.LEB:
Then Yahweh saw, and he spurned them,
    because of the provocation of his sons and his daughters.
texts.AMP: “The LORD saw it, and rejected them, Out of indignation with His sons and His daughters.
texts.NLT:
“The Lord saw this and drew back,
    provoked to anger by his own sons and daughters.
texts.AKJV:
And when the Lord saw it, he abhorred them,
because of the provoking of his sons, and of his daughters.
texts.NRSV:
The Lord saw it and was jealous;
    he spurned his sons and daughters.

texts.NIV84: "I will hide my face from them," he said, "and see what their end will be; for they are a perverse generation, children who are unfaithful.
texts.KJV: And he said, I will hide my face from them, I will see what their end shall be: for they are a very froward generation, children in whom is no faith.
texts.NASB95:
“Then He said, ‘I will hide My face from them,
I will see what their end shall be;
For they are a perverse generation,
Sons in whom is no faithfulness.
texts.NKJV:
And He said: ‘I will hide My face from them,
I will see what their end will be,
For they are a perverse generation,
Children in whom is no faith.
texts.DRB: And he said: I will hide my face from them, and will consider what their last end shall be: for it is a perverse generation, and unfaithful children.
texts.AMP: “Then He said, ‘I will hide My face from them, I will see what their end shall be; For they are a perverse generation, Sons in whom there is no faithfulness.
texts.NLT:
He said, ‘I will abandon them;
    then see what becomes of them.
For they are a twisted generation,
    children without integrity.
texts.AKJV:
And he said, I will hide my face from them,
I will see what their end shall be:
for they are a very froward generation,
children in whom is no faith.

texts.NIV84: They are a nation without sense, there is no discernment in them.
texts.KJV: For they are a nation void of counsel, neither is there any understanding in them.
texts.NASB95:
“For they are a nation lacking in counsel,
And there is no understanding in them.
texts.NKJV:
“For they are a nation void of counsel,
Nor is there any understanding in them.
texts.DRB: They are a nation without counsel, and without wisdom.
texts.ESV:
“For they are a nation void of counsel,
    and there is no understanding in them.
texts.LEB:
For they are a nation void of sense,
    and there is not any understanding in them.
texts.AMP: “For they are a nation devoid of counsel, And there is no understanding in them.
texts.NLT:
“But Israel is a senseless nation;
    the people are foolish, without understanding.
texts.AKJV:
For they are a nation void of counsel,
neither is there any understanding in them.
texts.NRSV:
They are a nation void of sense;
    there is no understanding in them.

texts.NIV84: If only they were wise and would understand this and discern what their end will be!
texts.KJV: O that they were wise, that they understood this, that they would consider their latter end!
texts.NASB95:
“Would that they were wise, that they understood this,
That they would discern their future!
texts.NKJV:
Oh, that they were wise, that they understood this,
That they would consider their latter end!
texts.DRB: O that they would be wise and would understand, and would provide for their last end.
texts.ESV:
If they were wise, they would understand this;
    they would discern their latter end!
texts.LEB:
If only they were wise, they would understand this;
    they would discern for themselves their end.
texts.AMP: “O that they were wise, that they understood this, That they could discern their future and ultimate fate!
texts.NLT:
Oh, that they were wise and could understand this!
    Oh, that they might know their fate!
texts.AKJV:
O that they were wise, that they understood this,
that they would consider their latter end!
texts.NRSV:
If they were wise, they would understand this;
    they would discern what their end would be.

blocks.title: Their rock not like our Rock

texts.NIV84: For their rock is not like our Rock, as even our enemies concede.
texts.KJV: For their rock is not as our Rock, even our enemies themselves being judges.
texts.NASB95:
“Indeed their rock is not like our Rock,
Even our enemies themselves judge this.
texts.NKJV:
For their rock is not like our Rock,
Even our enemies themselves being judges.
texts.DRB: For our God is not as their gods: our enemies themselves are judges.
texts.ESV:
For their rock is not as our Rock;
    our enemies are by themselves.
texts.LEB:
For the fact of the matter is,
    their rock is not like our Rock,
    and our enemies recognize this.
texts.AMP: “For their rock is not like our Rock, Even our enemies themselves judge this.
texts.NLT:
But the rock of our enemies is not like our Rock,
    as even they recognize.
texts.AKJV:
For their rock is not as our Rock,
even our enemies themselves being judges.
texts.NRSV:
Indeed, their rock is not like our Rock;
    our enemies are fools.

texts.NIV84: Their vine comes from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes are filled with poison, and their clusters with bitterness.
texts.KJV: For their vine is of the vine of Sodom, and of the fields of Gomorrah: their grapes are grapes of gall, their clusters are bitter:
texts.NASB95:
“For their vine is from the vine of Sodom,
And from the fields of Gomorrah;
Their grapes are grapes of poison,
Their clusters, bitter.
texts.NKJV:
For their vine is of the vine of Sodom
And of the fields of Gomorrah;
Their grapes are grapes of gall,
Their clusters are bitter.
texts.DRB: Their vines are of the vineyard of Sodom, and of the suburbs of Gomorrha: their grapes are grapes of gall, and their clusters most bitter.
texts.AMP: “For their vine is from the vine of Sodom, And from the fields of Gomorrah; Their grapes are grapes of poison, Their clusters, bitter.
texts.NLT:
Their vine grows from the vine of Sodom,
    from the vineyards of Gomorrah.
Their grapes are poison,
    and their clusters are bitter.
texts.AKJV:
For their vine is of the vine of Sodom,
and of the fields of Gomorrah:
their grapes are grapes of gall,
their clusters are bitter:

texts.NIV84: Their wine is the venom of serpents, the deadly poison of cobras.
texts.KJV: Their wine is the poison of dragons, and the cruel venom of asps.
texts.NASB95:
“Their wine is the venom of serpents,
And the deadly poison of cobras.
texts.NKJV:
Their wine is the poison of serpents,
And the cruel venom of cobras.
texts.DRB: Their wine is the gall of dragons, and the venom of asps, which is incurable.
texts.ESV:
their wine is the poison of serpents
    and the cruel venom of asps.
texts.LEB:
Their wine is the poison of snakes,
    and the deadly poison of horned vipers.
texts.AMP: “Their wine is the venom of serpents, And the deadly poison of vipers.
texts.NLT:
Their wine is the venom of serpents,
    the deadly poison of cobras.
texts.AKJV:
their wine is the poison of dragons,
and the cruel venom of asps.
texts.NRSV:
their wine is the poison of serpents,
    the cruel venom of asps.

texts.NIV84: It is mine to avenge; I will repay. In due time their foot will slip; their day of disaster is near and their doom rushes upon them."
texts.KJV: To me belongeth vengeance and recompence; their foot shall slide in due time: for the day of their calamity is at hand, and the things that shall come upon them make haste.
texts.NASB95:
‘Vengeance is Mine, and retribution,
In due time their foot will slip;
For the day of their calamity is near,
And the impending things are hastening upon them.’
texts.DRB: Revenge is mine, and I will repay them in due time, that their foot may slide: the day of destruction is at hand, and the time makes haste to come.
texts.LEB:
Vengeance belongs to me and also recompense,
    for at the time their foot slips,
because the day of their disaster is near,
    and fate comes quickly for them.’
texts.AMP: ‘Vengeance is Mine, and retribution, In due time their foot will slip; For the day of their disaster is at hand, And their doom hurries to meet them.’
texts.NLT:
I will take revenge; I will pay them back.
    In due time their feet will slip.
Their day of disaster will arrive,
    and their destiny will overtake them.’

texts.NIV84: The LORD will judge his people and have compassion on his servants when he sees their strength is gone and no one is left, slave or free.
texts.KJV: For the Lord shall judge his people, and repent himself for his servants, when he seeth that their power is gone, and there is none shut up, or left.
texts.NASB95:
“For the Lord will vindicate His people,
And will have compassion on His servants,
When He sees that their strength is gone,
And there is none remaining, bond or free.
texts.NKJV:
“For the Lord will judge His people
And have compassion on His servants,
When He sees that their power is gone,
And there is no one remaining, bond or free.
texts.DRB: The Lord will judge his people, and will have mercy on his servants : he shall see that their hand is weakened, and that they who were shut up have also failed, and they that remained are consumed.
texts.ESV:
For the Lord will vindicate his people
    and have compassion on his servants,
when he sees that their power is gone
    and there is none remaining, bond or free.
texts.LEB:
For Yahweh will judge on behalf of his people,
    and concerning his servants;
he will change his mind when he sees that their power has disappeared,
    and there is no one left, confined or free.
texts.AMP: “For the LORD will vindicate His people, And will have compassion on His servants, When He sees that their strength (hand) is gone, And none remains, whether bond or free.
texts.NLT:
“Indeed, the Lord will give justice to his people,
    and he will change his mind about his servants,
when he sees their strength is gone
    and no one is left, slave or free.
texts.AKJV:
For the Lord shall judge his people,
and repent himself for his servants,
when he seeth that their power is gone,
and there is none shut up, or left.
texts.NRSV:
Indeed, the Lord will vindicate his people,
    have compassion on his servants,
when he sees that their power is gone,
    neither bond nor free remaining.

blocks.title: The Lord will sharpen his sword

texts.NIV84: "See now that I myself am He! There is no god besides me. I put to death and I bring to life, I have wounded and I will heal, and no one can deliver out of my hand.
texts.KJV: See now that I, even I, am he, and there is no god with me: I kill, and I make alive; I wound, and I heal: neither is there any that can deliver out of my hand.
texts.NASB95:
‘See now that I, I am He,
And there is no god besides Me;
It is I who put to death and give life.
I have wounded and it is I who heal,
And there is no one who can deliver from My hand.
texts.DRB: See ye that I alone am, and there is no other God besides me: I will kill and I will make to live: I will strike, and I will heal, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand.
texts.AMP: ‘See now that I, I am He, And there is no god besides Me; It is I who put to death and I who give life. I have wounded and it is I who heal, And there is no one who can deliver from [the power of] My hand.
texts.AKJV:
See now that I, even I, am he,
and there is no god with me:
I kill, and I make alive;
I wound, and I heal:
neither is there any that can deliver out of my hand.

texts.NIV84: I lift my hand to heaven and declare: As surely as I live forever,
texts.KJV: For I lift up my hand to heaven, and say, I live for ever.
texts.NASB95:
‘Indeed, I lift up My hand to heaven,
And say, as I live forever,
texts.NKJV:
For I raise My hand to heaven,
And say, “As I live forever,
texts.DRB: I will lift up my hand to heaven, and I will say: I live for ever.
texts.ESV:
For I lift up my hand to heaven
    and swear, As I live forever,
texts.LEB:
For indeed I lift up my hand to heaven,
    And I promise as I live forever,
texts.AMP: ‘Indeed, I lift up My hand to heaven, And say (swear an oath), as I live forever,
texts.NLT:
Now I raise my hand to heaven
    and declare, “As surely as I live,
texts.AKJV:
For I lift up my hand to heaven,
and say, I live for ever.
texts.NRSV:
For I lift up my hand to heaven
    and swear, As I live forever,

texts.NIV84: when I sharpen my flashing sword and my hand grasps it in judgment, I will take vengeance on my adversaries and repay those who hate me.
texts.KJV: If I whet my glittering sword, and mine hand take hold on judgment; I will render vengeance to mine enemies, and will reward them that hate me.
texts.NASB95:
If I sharpen My flashing sword,
And My hand takes hold on justice,
I will render vengeance on My adversaries,
And I will repay those who hate Me.
texts.NKJV:
If I whet My glittering sword,
And My hand takes hold on judgment,
I will render vengeance to My enemies,
And repay those who hate Me.
texts.DRB: If I shall whet my sword as the lightning, and my hand take hold on judgment: I will render vengeance to my enemies, and repay them that hate me.
texts.ESV:
if I sharpen my flashing sword
    and my hand takes hold on judgment,
I will take vengeance on my adversaries
    and will repay those who hate me.
texts.LEB:
When I sharpen my flashing sword,
    and my hand takes hold of it in judgment,
I will take reprisals against my foes,
    and my haters I will repay.
texts.AMP: If I sharpen the lightning of My sword, And My hand takes hold of judgment, I will render vengeance on My adversaries, And I will repay those who hate Me.
texts.NLT:
when I sharpen my flashing sword
    and begin to carry out justice,
I will take revenge on my enemies
    and repay those who reject me.
texts.AKJV:
If I whet my glittering sword,
and mine hand take hold on judgment;
I will render vengeance to mine enemies,
and will reward them that hate me.
texts.NRSV:
when I whet my flashing sword
    and my hand takes hold on judgment,
I will take vengeance on my adversaries
    and will repay those who hate me.

texts.NIV84: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, while my sword devours flesh: the blood of the slain and the captives, the heads of the enemy leaders."
texts.KJV: I will make mine arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh; and that with the blood of the slain and of the captives, from the beginning of revenges upon the enemy.
texts.NASB95:
‘I will make My arrows drunk with blood,
And My sword will devour flesh,
With the blood of the slain and the captives,
From the long-haired leaders of the enemy.’
texts.NKJV:
I will make My arrows drunk with blood,
And My sword shall devour flesh,
With the blood of the slain and the captives,
From the heads of the leaders of the enemy.” ’
texts.DRB: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh, of the blood of the slain and of the captivity, of the bare head of the enemies.
texts.ESV:
I will make my arrows drunk with blood,
    and my sword shall devour flesh—
with the blood of the slain and the captives,
    from the long-haired heads of the enemy.’
texts.LEB:
I will make my arrows drunk with blood,
    and my sword shall devour flesh with the blood of the slain,
    and captives from the heads of the leaders of the enemy.’
texts.AMP: ‘I will make My arrows drunk with blood, And My sword will devour flesh, With the blood of the slain and the captives, From the heads of the leaders of the enemy.’
texts.NLT:
I will make my arrows drunk with blood,
    and my sword will devour flesh—
the blood of the slaughtered and the captives,
    and the heads of the enemy leaders.”’
texts.AKJV:
I will make mine arrows drunk with blood,
and my sword shall devour flesh;
and that with the blood of the slain and of the captives,
from the beginning of revenges upon the enemy.
texts.NRSV:
I will make my arrows drunk with blood,
    and my sword shall devour flesh—
with the blood of the slain and the captives,
    from the long-haired enemy.’

texts.NIV84: Rejoice, O nations, with his people,, for he will avenge the blood of his servants; he will take vengeance on his enemies and make atonement for his land and people.
texts.KJV: Rejoice, O ye nations, with his people: for he will avenge the blood of his servants, and will render vengeance to his adversaries, and will be merciful unto his land, and to his people.
texts.NASB95:
“Rejoice, O nations, with His people;
For He will avenge the blood of His servants,
And will render vengeance on His adversaries,
And will atone for His land and His people.”
texts.DRB: Praise his people, ye nations, for he will revenge the blood of his servants: and will render vengeance to their enemies, and he will be merciful to the land of his people.
texts.ESV:
“Rejoice with him, O heavens;
    bow down to him, all gods,
for he avenges the blood of his children
    and takes vengeance on his adversaries.
He repays those who hate him
    and cleanses his people's land.”
texts.LEB:
Call for songs of joy, O nations, concerning his people,
    for the blood of his servants he will avenge,
and he will take reprisals against his foes,
    and he will make atonement for his land, his people.”
texts.AMP: “Rejoice, O nations, with His people; For He will avenge the blood of His servants, And will render vengeance on His adversaries, And will atone for His land and His people.”
texts.NLT:
“Rejoice with him, you heavens,
    and let all of God’s angels worship him.
Rejoice with his people, you Gentiles,
    and let all the angels be strengthened in him.
For he will avenge the blood of his children;
    he will take revenge against his enemies.
He will repay those who hate him
    and cleanse his people’s land.”
texts.AKJV:
Rejoice, O ye nations, with his people:
for he will avenge the blood of his servants,
and will render vengeance to his adversaries,
and will be merciful unto his land, and to his people.

texts.NIV84: Moses came with Joshua son of Nun and spoke all the words of this song in the hearing of the people.
texts.KJV: And Moses came and spake all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he, and Hoshea the son of Nun.
texts.NASB95: Then Moses came and spoke all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he, with Joshua the son of Nun.
texts.NKJV: So Moses came with Joshua the son of Nun and spoke all the words of this song in the hearing of the people.
texts.DRB: So Moses came and spoke all the words of this canticle in the ears of the people, and Josue the son of Nun.
texts.ESV: Moses came and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Joshua the son of Nun.
texts.LEB: And Moses came, and he spoke all the words of this song in the ears of the people; that is, he and Joshua the son of Nun.
texts.AMP: Then Moses came and spoke all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Joshua the son of Nun.
texts.NLT: So Moses came with Joshua son of Nun and recited all the words of this song to the people.
texts.AKJV: And Moses came and spake all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he, and Hoshea the son of Nun.
texts.NRSV: Moses came and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people, he and Joshua son of Nun.
